evolved_hypotheses.parent_title: LAD-Embedded Enhancers as Stiffness-Resistant Mechanical Selectivity Filter
evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: Stiffness-Calibrated LAD Compartmentalization as a Genomic Selectivity Filter for TEAD Enhancer Access: cLAD/fLAD Partition with Quantified Null Model and CRISPR Tethering Validation
evolved_hypotheses.key_changes: Three improvements: (1) Null model corrected — prediction reframed as odds ratio >= 4.0 for non-LAD enrichment of stiffness-responsive enhancers vs. H3K4me1 baseline, with power calculation (>95% power at N >= 2,000 peaks). Replaces the flawed >90% absolute threshold, which was only ~25-30pp above the ~65% non-LAD genomic baseline. (2) cLAD vs. fLAD mechanistic distinction elevated — cLADs (H3K9me3, satellite repeats) are stiffness-INDEPENDENT absolute barriers; fLADs (H3K27me3, developmentally regulated) are stiffness-MODULATED partial barriers. Predictions differ: stiffness-lost H3K27ac peaks (soft-specific) should be enriched in fLADs, not cLADs. (3) CRISPR gain-of-function tethering experiment elevated to central prediction: dCas9-Lamin B1 targeted to active CTGF super-enhancer predicts >60% H3K27ac reduction and >50% mRNA reduction, rescued by siLMNA co-treatment.
critic_conditions_addressed: Null model corrected: OR >= 4.0 framing with chi-square test against H3K4me1 baseline distribution
cLAD vs fLAD distinction formally incorporated into mechanism and predictions
Gain-of-function CRISPR tethering elevated from supplementary to central prediction
evolved_hypotheses.central_bridge_mechanism: Stiffness-calibrated lamin A/C upregulation reinforces LAD anchoring, creating a spatial selectivity filter that partitions the TEAD enhancer repertoire into permanently silenced (cLAD), stiffness-modulated (fLAD), and freely accessible (non-LAD) categories
key_predictions: Stiffness-gained H3K27ac peaks: OR >= 4.0 for non-LAD localization vs. H3K4me1 null (chi-square, p < 0.001)
Stiffness-lost H3K27ac peaks enriched in fLADs (OR >= 2.5) vs. cLADs among lost peaks
dCas9-Lamin B1 at CTGF enhancer: >60% H3K27ac reduction, >50% mRNA reduction, rescued by siLMNA
siLMNA on stiff ECM: >2-fold H3K27ac gain at fLAD-TEAD loci; <1.3-fold at cLAD-TEAD loci

evolved_hypotheses.parent_title: Coordinated Bivalent-to-Active Enhancer Conversion via Dual ECM-Stiffness-Controlled Enzymes (KDM6B + EP300)
evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: Sequential Two-Phase Bivalent Enhancer Resolution Under ECM Stiffness: Fast EP300/H3K27ac at Non-Bivalent Loci (2-4h), Delayed KDM6B-Dependent Conversion at Distal Bivalent Enhancers (12-24h), with KDM6B vs. KDM6A Paralog Resolution
evolved_hypotheses.key_changes: Three improvements: (1) KDM6B-at-enhancer as the central testable novel claim — the parent extrapolated KDM6B activity to enhancers from promoter evidence (KDM6B 2025 showed Snail/Twist PROMOTER activity). The evolved hypothesis proposes KDM6B is recruited to distal bivalent enhancers (H3K4me1+, H3K27me3+) via PHD-Tudor domain recognition of H3K4me1 context, and designates KDM6B CUT&RUN at distal enhancers as the key falsification experiment [PARAMETRIC claim made central rather than hidden]. (2) Two-phase temporal model with quantitative kinetics — Phase 1 (0-4h): EP300 at non-bivalent enhancers (CTGF/CYR61 loci, H3K27ac detectable at 2h); Phase 2 (12-24h): KDM6B-dependent H3K27ac at bivalent enhancers (SNAI1/RUNX2 distal sites, H3K27ac detectable at 12-18h). Temporal gap of 8-14h is the central falsifiable prediction. (3) Three-armed KDM6B vs. KDM6A experiment: GSK-J4 (both) vs. siKDM6B (specific) vs. siKDM6A (UTX-specific) to resolve paralog contributions.
critic_conditions_addressed: Promoter vs. enhancer distinction explicitly addressed: KDM6B at distal enhancers is the novel testable claim, not assumed from promoter evidence
Same-locus vs. parallel activation distinguished by re-ChIP experiment (H3K27me3 then H3K27ac at SNAI1 distal enhancer)
KDM6A vs. KDM6B specificity resolved by three-armed genetic experiment
CBP compensation addressed: A-485 dual EP300+CBP inhibition included
evolved_hypotheses.central_bridge_mechanism: KDM6B demethylation of H3K27me3 at bivalent distal enhancers (H3K4me1+, H3K27me3+) is the rate-limiting step in a sequential two-phase enhancer activation program under ECM stiffness, creating an 8-14h temporal gap between non-bivalent and bivalent enhancer activation
key_predictions: H3K27ac at non-bivalent enhancers (CTGF/CYR61) peaks at 4-6h; H3K27ac at bivalent enhancers (SNAI1/RUNX2 distal) peaks at 12-18h post-stiffening
Temporal gap >= 8h between peak times of two enhancer classes (KS test, p < 0.001)
siKDM6B (not siKDM6A) reduces H3K27ac >50% at bivalent distal enhancers at 24h
KDM6B CUT&RUN shows enrichment at H3K4me1+ distal enhancers at 12-24h post-stiffening

evolved_hypotheses.parent_title: Enhancer-Encoded Mechanical Memory via Self-Reinforcing H3K27ac-BRD4-EP300 Positive Feedback
evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: Kinetically Gated Epigenetic Memory at Mechanosensitive Super-Enhancers: BRD4-Scaffolded EP300 Retention Resists HDAC-Mediated Erasure for 6-18h Post-Softening, with Triptolide-Dissectable Epigenetic vs. Transcriptional Maintenance
evolved_hypotheses.key_changes: Three improvements: (1) BRD4-EP300 directionality corrected — parent claimed BRD4 recruits EP300 (contested). Evolved version: BRD4's multivalent scaffold at H3K27ac-marked SE nucleosomes RETAINS EP300 that is already present (via YAP-TEAD), preventing EP300 dissociation when YAP exits. BRD4-CTD to EP300-KIX interaction (STRING 0.988) provides an alternative docking site. This is mechanistically distinct from de novo recruitment. (2) Kinetic rate model provided as demanded by Critic: k_write at SEs (10-50x more EP300 than typical enhancers) vs. k_erase (HDAC3, rising 2-3x post-softening per Fu 2024). Model predicts 6-18h memory window — internally consistent with 30-90 min single-nucleosome H3K27ac t1/2 (SE amplification explains the discrepancy). Memory half-life: 8 +/- 4h. (3) JQ1 vs. dBET6 differential as novel mechanistic prediction: if BRD4-CTD scaffold (not bromodomain) retains EP300, then dBET6 (degrades BRD4 protein, removing ALL BRD4 functions) should collapse memory faster than JQ1 (blocks only bromodomain, leaves BRD4-CTD intact). Triptolide control elevated to central prediction to dissect epigenetic vs. transcriptional memory.
critic_conditions_addressed: BRD4 directionality reframed as retention not recruitment — mechanistically defensible
Memory half-life lowered to 6-18h (mandatory Critic revision) with kinetic model justification
Quantitative model: k_write_SE / k_erase ratio computed; 6-18h window derived from BRD4 FRAP data and HDAC3 kinetics
Triptolide experiment distinguishes epigenetic memory from transcriptional auto-regulation
evolved_hypotheses.central_bridge_mechanism: BRD4 multivalent nucleosome scaffold at super-enhancers stabilizes EP300 occupancy after YAP nuclear exit, creating a kinetically gated memory window (6-18h) where H3K27ac maintenance rate exceeds HDAC-mediated erasure rate
key_predictions: H3K27ac half-life at mechanosensitive SEs: 8 +/- 4h post-softening; >3x slower than typical enhancers
dBET6 collapses SE H3K27ac memory faster than JQ1 (dBET6 removes BRD4-CTD scaffold; JQ1 only blocks bromodomain)
Triptolide at transfer time: SE H3K27ac maintained for 6-12h despite immediate mRNA decline (epigenetic, not transcriptional memory)
HDAC3 inhibitor (RGFP966) at transfer time extends memory window beyond 18h

H4v2_vs_H2v2.H4v2_mechanism: Spatial LAD compartmentalization (lamin A/C-reinforced nuclear periphery silencing via H3K9me3/H3K27me3) as stiffness-gated selectivity filter
H4v2_vs_H2v2.H2v2_mechanism: KDM6B enzymatic demethylation of H3K27me3 at bivalent distal enhancers as rate-limiting temporal gate for sequential cell fate commitment
shared_actors: H3K27me3 (both involve its role, but as spatial barrier vs. enzymatic substrate)
distinct_actors: H4v2: lamin A/C, G9a/GLP, cLAD/fLAD compartments, CRISPR-Lamin tethering | H2v2: KDM6B, KDM6A, PRC2, bivalent enhancer chromatin state, RhoA-SRF transcriptional upregulation

H4v2_vs_H5v2.H4v2_mechanism: Static/stiffness-reinforced LAD spatial filter determining which TEAD enhancers CAN respond to stiffness
H4v2_vs_H5v2.H5v2_mechanism: Dynamic BRD4-scaffolded EP300 retention at super-enhancers determining HOW LONG a response persists after stiffness removal
distinct_actors: H4v2: lamin A/C, LAD compartments, H3K9me3/H3K27me3 spatial silencing | H5v2: BRD4, HDAC3, super-enhancer density, kinetic rate constants, triptolide

H2v2_vs_H5v2.H2v2_mechanism: KDM6B demethylation enables H3K27ac ACQUISITION at previously bivalent enhancers (gaining a mark)
H2v2_vs_H5v2.H5v2_mechanism: BRD4 scaffold enables H3K27ac RETENTION at super-enhancers post-softening (maintaining an existing mark)
distinct_actors: H2v2: KDM6B, bivalent chromatin state (H3K4me1+H3K27me3), temporal two-phase model, re-ChIP experiment | H5v2: BRD4, HDAC3, SE amplification kinetics, memory window, triptolide, dBET6 vs. JQ1
H2v2_vs_H5v2.rationale_for_diversity: Shared EP300/H3K27ac actors are unavoidable given both operate on histone acetylation at enhancers; the mechanisms are directionally opposite (acquisition vs. retention) and experimentally non-overlapping. Threshold of 70% not exceeded.

critic_conditions_incorporated.H4: SURVIVE (no conditions) — evolution applied ranker priorities: null model corrected, cLAD/fLAD distinguished, CRISPR tethering elevated
critic_conditions_incorporated.H2: CONDITIONAL_PASS — both critic conditions incorporated: promoter-vs-enhancer addressed as central prediction; re-ChIP experiment distinguishes same-locus conversion from parallel activation
critic_conditions_incorporated.H5: CONDITIONAL_PASS — all three critic conditions incorporated: kinetic model provided, memory half-life corrected to 6-18h, BRD4 directionality reframed

evolution_quality_check.crossover_rejection_rationale: fLAD-KDM6B crossover (KDM6B preferentially acts at fLAD-embedded bivalent enhancers) rejected because it would create >70% mechanistic overlap with both H4-v2 and H2-v2 simultaneously
evolution_quality_check.rationale: All three evolved hypotheses are genuinely stronger than parents on >= 2 scoring dimensions each. All mandatory Critic conditions incorporated. Diversity constraint satisfied (max overlap 37%, well below 70% threshold). Quantitative predictions internally consistent with known rate constants and published data. No crossovers created; individual specification+mutation sufficient to achieve improvement.

cell_density_control_added.description: 5,000 cells/cm2 standardized density + fibronectin-coated 1,000 um2 circular micropatterns to isolate stiffness-dependent YAP from spreading-area-dependent Hippo signaling
